default.label: Akima Intent Profile
default.northStar: The harness must understand the user's ideal and ship work that does not feel AI-generated or template-derived.
default.qualityBar: Do not stop at passing checks. The outcome must feel more convincing, more deliberate, and more real than the stated benchmark.
mustHaves: Intent is translated into explicit acceptance checks before implementation.
Outputs have hierarchy, density, and material realness.
Benchmark strengths are named and surpassed intentionally.
If visual quality is weak, the task is not complete.
Prefer restrained light palettes: warm off-white or cool blue-gray surfaces with clear hierarchy and operational calm.
Color systems must feel intentional through contrast, hierarchy, and material depth, not decorative dark mode or broad brand blocking.
The preferred HarnesUI direction is calm or crisp light UI, but it needs a stronger designed signature than lightly tinted panels.
Repeated operational items such as chats must have readable boundaries, clear selection state, and enough contrast to feel like distinct rooms or records.
avoid: abstract copy with no concrete proof
dark full-console palettes that make the product feel heavy or developer-only
warm brown/orange brand palettes that look rustic or amateur instead of precise
notes: Build, tests, and HTTP 200 are necessary but never sufficient.
Workspace targeting mistakes must be treated as hard failures.
2026-05-01 HarnesUI theme scoring: quiet-lux and ops-blue were 65/100; console and product-bold were 40/100.
2026-05-01 second HarnesUI pass: calm-ops, crisp-ledger, and blue-steel were around 60/100; calm-ops or crisp-ledger were only slightly better at 65/100.

2026-05-01 third HarnesUI pass: northline was the least bad direction; blue-gray shell is preferred, but the central chat and conversation surface must also feel blue-gray rather than disconnected white.

2026-05-01 HarnesUI failure mode: small requested color tweaks stay under 65/100; the assistant must make opinionated layout, hierarchy, and material-depth decisions instead of only following explicit color comments.
2026-05-01 HarnesUI scoring: the northline director pass reached about 68/100; next improvement should target beautiful whole-layout composition beyond color.
2026-05-01 HarnesUI detail feedback: the chat list felt unclear because item boundaries were too weak; future passes should treat boundary clarity as visual quality, not mere decoration.

autonomy.primaryObjective: Realize the user's intended outcome with minimal intervention from the user.
